ncert_truth_statements: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL): the algebraic sum of currents meeting at any junction in a circuit is zero, based on conservation of charge
Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL): the algebraic sum of changes in potential around any closed loop in a circuit is zero, based on conservation of energy
Current flowing into a junction must equal the current flowing out of that junction at every instant
In applying KVL, a potential drop of IR occurs across a resistor when traversed in the direction of the current
Kirchhoff's laws apply to any network of resistors and EMF sources, enabling calculation of currents in complex circuits
